query: When was the first battle royale game made?

This game plays similarly to another Bandai Gundam game for the Sony PlayStation 2, "". Gundam Battle Royale is a game released for the PlayStation Portable. "Battlegrounds" is considered the defining game of the battle royale game genre due to its popularity, even though other games, including Greene's previous mods, were already on the market. The novel was translated into English by Yuji Oniki and published by Viz Media in 2003. An expanded English edition was later published by Haika Soru, a division of Viz Media, in 2009. In May 2012, in an interview to Digital Trends, game director Omar Kendall was asked about the possibility of PlayStation Vita version of "PlayStation All-Stars Battle Royale", and he simply replied, "Maybe... stay tuned." A Vita version was announced at the Electronic Entertainment Expo 2012, to be developed by Bluepoint Games, and was confirmed to feature cross-play with the PlayStation 3. In 1981, Gaines and eleven others played the first game of paintball, using Nel-Spot pistols which were intended for marking trees and livestock by ranchers. This initial game was a capture the flag style scenario. Later, Bob Gurnsey formed the National Survival Game company, which was the first firm to sell paintball equipment. In 1997 Gaines published a novel entitled "Survival Games" which features inventors of paintball, probably based upon Gaines and Gurnsey, as the main characters.  Answer:

baseline.final_answer: The novel "Battle Royale" was published in April 1999 and later made into a manga and film. The game "Gundam Battle Royale" was released in Japan and South Korea on October 5, 2006. "Battlegrounds" is considered the defining game of the battle royale genre, released in 2017. "PlayStation All-Stars Battle Royale" was revealed on April 26, 2012.

evaluation.feedback: The answer contains factual claims but lacks any citations, making it impossible to verify grounding. The query asks for the first battle royale game, but the answer provides multiple game release dates without identifying the first.
